---
title: "IDS/Ec/PS 126: Applied Data Analysis"
subtitle: "Example Slides"
author: "Prof. Jonathan N. Katz"
institute: "Caltech"
date: "2026-03-26"
format: caltech-revealjs
---

```{r}
#| include: false
#| cache: false
source("_extensions/caltech/caltech-setup.R")
```

## Key Concepts

- **Bayesian inference** updates prior beliefs with observed data
- The posterior distribution summarizes what we know after seeing the data
- Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) methods allow us to sample from complex posteriors
- Model checking is essential:
  - Prior predictive checks
  - Posterior predictive checks
  - Cross-validation
- Always report uncertainty alongside point estimates

## Outline

:::{.left-list}
1. First topic
2. Second topic
3. Third topic
:::

:::{.right-image}
![](_extensions/caltech/Caltech_LOGO-Orange_RGB.png){width="75%"}
:::

## Two-Column Layout

:::{.pull-left}
### Left Column

- Bullet point one
- Bullet point two
- **Bold text** in Caltech orange
:::

:::{.pull-right}
### Right Column

Some code:

```r
library(tidyverse)

mtcars |>
  group_by(cyl) |>
  summarize(mpg = mean(mpg))
```
:::

## Tables

| Variable | Mean | SD |
|----------|-----:|---:|
| mpg | 20.09 | 6.03 |
| hp | 146.69 | 68.56 |
| wt | 3.22 | 0.98 |

: Motor Trend car summary statistics

## Inverse Slide {.inverse}

This slide has an inverse color scheme.

- Orange background
- Cream text

## Header Background Slide {.header_background}

Content below the orange banner header.

- This layout places the heading in a fixed orange banner
- Content area is pushed down with extra padding

## Callouts And Notes

:::{.box-2}
Quick teaching prompt or code annotation.
:::

:::{.box-4}
Longer explanatory callout for datasets, examples, or setup notes.
:::

::: {.notes}
Mention that `.box-2` is meant for short prompts and `.box-4` for fuller callouts.
:::

## Code Size Utilities

:::{.tiny}
```r
# This code block uses the .tiny class (60% size)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Weight vs MPG", x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
       y = "Miles per Gallon", color = "Cylinders") +
  theme_minimal()
```
:::

## Small Code

:::{.small}
```r
# This code block uses the .small class (80% size)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
```
:::

## Color Utilities

[This text is in Caltech orange]{.base}

[This text is cream colored]{.white .bg-base}

> This is a blockquote with an orange left border.

## Math

Bayes' theorem:

$$
P(\theta \mid y) = \frac{P(y \mid \theta) \, P(\theta)}{P(y)}
$$

where $P(\theta \mid y)$ is the posterior distribution.

## R Helpers

```{r}
#| echo: false
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = orange_scheme[1:3]) +
  labs(color = "Cylinders") +
  theme_caltech()
```

## Image Carousel Helper

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
carousel_images <- rep("_extensions/caltech/Caltech_LOGO-Orange_RGB.png", 2)
caltech_image_carousel(
  carousel_images,
  captions = c("First image", "Second image"),
  alt = c("Caltech logo", "Caltech logo"),
  interval = 2000
)
```

## Incremental Reveals

First point is always visible.

. . .

This appears after a pause.

. . .

And this appears after another pause.

## Three-Column Layout

:::{.pull-left-3}
### Column 1
Content for the first column.
:::

:::{.pull-middle-3}
### Column 2
Content for the middle column.
:::

:::{.pull-right-3}
### Column 3
Content for the third column.
:::

## Footnotes

Some text with a footnote reference.

:::{.footnote}
This is a footnote positioned at the bottom of the slide.
:::

## Hide Logo Slide {.hide_logo}

The Caltech logo is hidden on this slide.

## Thank You {.inverse .center}

Questions?
